desc: Auto-populate ExecutionReport at terminal, compute gap trajectory across chain history, synthesize convergence assessment into chainReady output. Agents get 'converging at 40%/iteration' or 'stuck on 3 persistent gaps' — enough to write specs that converge rather than churn.

init.desc: Current state: asymptotic-chain DAG complete. chainReady replaces done:true. Scoring wired into TerminalBrief. detectGaps broadened. But ExecutionReport is opt-in, gap trajectory not computed, no convergence assessment.

auto-execution-report.desc: Auto-populate ExecutionReport when terminal node advances, without requiring --evaluate-file. Compute from data already available: nodesExecuted from CompletionStore (count of completed node IDs), totalDuration from trail metrics (totalWallClockMs), retriesPerNode from trail entries (count advance entries per node with detail.passed === false followed by retry). observations[] from handoff summaries (gotchas from all nodes). blockers[] from handoff summaries. deltaAssessment left empty (filled by gap-trajectory node). Wire this into advance.ts terminal path: when building ChainLink for appendLink(), auto-compute the ExecutionReport instead of requiring --evaluate-file. The --evaluate-file path remains as an override.

gap-trajectory.desc: Compute gap trajectory across chain history. New module src/lib/convergence/gap-trajectory.ts exports computeGapTrajectory(repoRoot) → GapTrajectory. Algorithm: (1) load head-index.json to get predecessor chain, (2) for each archived head in .roadmap/heads/, load the DAG and run detectGaps() on it, (3) build per-iteration gap counts by type, (4) compute resolved[] (gaps in predecessor not in current), persistent[] (gaps in both), new[] (gaps in current not in predecessor), (5) compute convergence direction: gap count trend (shrinking/stable/growing), reduction rate per iteration. GapTrajectory type: { iterations: Array<{ dagId, iteration, gapCount, gapsByType }>, resolved: GapEntry[], persistent: GapEntry[], new: GapEntry[], trend: 'converging' | 'stable' | 'diverging', reductionRate: number }. Pure function over archived DAG files — no dependency on current execution state.

convergence-assessment.desc: Synthesize gap trajectory + execution report into a ConvergenceAssessment and wire it into the chainReady output. New module src/lib/convergence/assessment.ts exports assessConvergence(trajectory, currentGaps, executionReport) → ConvergenceAssessment. ConvergenceAssessment type: { trend, reductionRate, persistentGaps[], resolvedThisIteration[], newThisIteration[], recommendation: string, iterationSummary: string }. The recommendation is a concrete sentence: 'Gap surface shrinking 40% per iteration — 3 persistent gaps need targeted shell validators' or 'Diverging after iteration 2 — 5 new no-shell-coverage gaps suggest validator strategy needs revision'. Wire into: (1) TerminalBrief gets a convergence field (both pure and IO versions), (2) advance.ts chainReady output includes convergenceAssessment, (3) the ExecutionReport.deltaAssessment auto-populates from the assessment's iterationSummary. Update src/runtime/brief.ts, src/lib/terminal-brief.ts, src/cli/advance.ts.

test-convergence.desc: Integration tests proving convergence assessment works across chain iterations. Test 1: computeGapTrajectory on a repo with 2 archived heads returns correct resolved/persistent/new classification. Test 2: assessConvergence with shrinking gap surface returns trend:'converging' with correct reductionRate. Test 3: assessConvergence with growing gap surface returns trend:'diverging'. Test 4: assessConvergence with zero chain history returns trend:'stable' (first iteration baseline). Test 5: terminal advance chainReady output includes convergenceAssessment field. Test 6: auto ExecutionReport populates nodesExecuted and totalDuration from trail data. Use mock archived heads (write temp DAG JSON files) for trajectory tests.

term.desc: Convergence-aware chain loop verified. Agents at terminal see: gap trajectory (resolved/persistent/new), convergence trend (converging/stable/diverging), reduction rate, and a concrete recommendation for the successor spec. ExecutionReport auto-populates. The full evaluative context for convergence-based spec authoring is in the chainReady output.
